proved: The product version rail subscribes to the same lifecycle-owned live Y.Doc as the Lexical editor instead of opening a second document or mirror.
Save current draft reads the exact semantic editor revision, requires the exact current version head, and rechecks the editor revision inside the final immutable-head transaction.
The rail lists verified content-addressed checkpoints with current-head, note, historical display name, time, and bounded hash metadata.
Selecting a checkpoint renders the deterministic parent change note and at most eight ordered block changes without loading or calling a model.

The server-rendered component contract includes an accessible version region, labeled optional-note input, explicit save action, selected state, current-head copy, and an honest read-only restore notice.
Store migration v3 rewrites version-2 saves once so the generated per-install participant ID becomes durable, preserves existing researcher identity/name, and rejects future store versions.
Live-document subscription tests cover initial absence, registration, strict-mode-safe replacement, final cleanup, and subscriber removal.
The local provider remains absent from the UI/MCP live-document registry until IndexedDB synchronization has completed, and disconnect removes it again.
The product rail rejects invalid stored records, a missing current head, and a selected checkpoint whose immutable parent is absent; failed verification clears stale rail state and disables saving.
notProved: Packaged pointer, keyboard, focus, narrow-layout, or dark-theme interaction for the version rail.
Safe product restore; the domain restore creates a new immutable head but does not replace the active Lexical document, so restore remains unavailable in the UI.
Authenticated or cryptographically signed participant identity; the per-install ID and editable display name are attribution metadata only.
Two-machine or Drive-backed version convergence, non-head branch reconciliation, archive export, or portable verification.
Semantic usefulness of the deterministic diff for every document structure; non-policy prose still lacks stable block identity.
A release/updater build containing this development checkpoint.
